subject: biology
title: 19M.1.HL.TZ2.35
question_html:
What are features of both endoskeletons of mammals and exoskeletons of insects?

A. They are both made of bone.

B. They both have cartilage.

C. They are both moved by antagonistic sets of muscles.

D. Both consist of dead tissue.
examiners_html: This was an easy question. One comment mentions that not all bones or parts of the insect exoskeleton are moved. The question focuses on common features; the fact that they are moved by muscles does not imply that they are all moved.
